talk_title: Beyond the Private Chatbot: Bringing AI to Collaborative Work
talk_abstract:
AI has reshaped individual productivity. We write, research, code,
and ramp up faster than ever. But collaborative workflows haven't
kept pace. Teams still run on pre-AI processes while each person
builds richer context inside their own private chatbot. The result
is a paradox: individuals get more capable as shared context grows
more fragmented.

This talk explores the practical patterns for building collaborative
AI systems through shared skills, context design, and team-level
workflow changes that make AI useful for the whole organization,
not just one person.

talk_title: The Greenfield Delusion: Shipping AI Inside Real Organizations
talk_abstract:
Everyone can now build impressive AI demos in a weekend. Almost none of
that work ever makes it into production at a real organization. This
session walks through the three questions every deployment dies on, then
puts the framework to work: we'll deploy an AI agent into an existing
codebase in real time, not a blank folder. The gap between demo and
production, made concrete.

talk_title: AI as a Teammate: Driving Double-Digit Engineering Productivity Gains
talk_abstract:
AI is no longer just a tool for automation. It is becoming an active
teammate in the software development lifecycle. By embedding AI into
daily workflows, teams can reduce time spent on repetitive tasks,
accelerate code reviews, and improve decision quality through faster
access to context and insights. This talk explores how treating AI as
a collaborative partner, rather than a passive assistant, drives
measurable efficiency gains while maintaining code quality and
engineering rigor.

talk_title: Tradeoffs and Learnings with AI Agent Self-Signups
talk_abstract:
As AI agents increasingly become first-class actors on the internet, every
existing auth model breaks down, they all assume a logged-in human at the
start of the flow, but agents now arrive at endpoints before any human
does. At Inkbox, Ray and his team recently enabled agent self-registration,
which forced them to rethink identity, trust, and permissions from the
ground up. A lesson from one of the first production teams solving this
problem.
